questions.stem_en: A solid white line on the right side of the road marks:
questions.explanation_en: A solid white line on the right side of the road marks the edge of the roadway. It helps drivers see the road boundary, especially at night or in poor weather.
questions.explanation_zh: 道路右侧的实线白线标记路面边缘。它帮助驾驶员看到道路边界，尤其是在夜间或恶劣天气中。
questions.explanation_zhHant: 道路右側的實線白線標記路面邊緣。它幫助駕駛員看到道路邊界，尤其是在夜間或惡劣天氣中。
questions.stem_zhHant: 道路右側的白色實線標記：

questions.stem_en: When a traffic signal is not working at an intersection, you should treat it as:

options.text_en: A green light — proceed with caution
A stop sign in all directions
An uncontrolled intersection — fastest vehicle goes first
options.text_zhHant: 無控制路口——速度快的車輛先行

questions.explanation_en: When a traffic signal is not working, treat the intersection as if there are stop signs in all directions. Stop, then proceed when it is safe.
questions.explanation_zh: 当交通信号灯不工作时，将交叉路口视为各个方向都有停车标志。停车，然后在安全时通行。
questions.explanation_zhHant: 當交通信號燈不工作時，將交叉路口视為各個方向都有停車標誌。停車，然後在安全時通行。
questions.stem_zhHant: 路口的交通信號燈不工作時，你應該視其為：

questions.stem_en: Double solid white lines on the road indicate:

options.text_en: You may change lanes with caution
A lane barrier — do not cross or change lanes
options.text_zhHant: 車道屏障——禁止穿越或變道

options.text_en: The road is a one-way street

questions.explanation_en: Double solid white lines indicate a lane barrier. You may not cross them to change lanes. They are often found near freeway on/off ramps and between regular lanes and HOV lanes.
questions.explanation_zh: 双实线白线表示车道屏障。你不能越过它们变道。它们通常出现在高速公路入口/出口匝道附近以及普通车道和HOV车道之间。
questions.explanation_zhHant: 雙實線白線表示車道屏障。你不能越過它们變道。它们通常出現在高速公路入口/出口匝道附近以及普通車道和HOV車道之間。
questions.stem_zhHant: 道路上的雙實白線表示：

questions.stem_en: A yield line made of solid white triangles on the road tells you to:

options.text_en: Yield or stop for approaching traffic

questions.explanation_en: A yield line is a solid white line of triangles pointing toward approaching vehicles. It shows where to yield or stop before proceeding.
questions.explanation_zh: 让行线是由指向来车方向的三角形组成的实线白线。它指示在哪里让行或停车后再通行。
questions.explanation_zhHant: 讓行線是由指向來車方向的三角形組成的實線白線。它指示在哪裡讓行或停車後再通行。
questions.stem_zhHant: 由白色實心三角形組成的讓行線告訴你：

questions.stem_en: Large broken lines painted on a freeway lane indicate:

options.text_en: The lane is for HOV vehicles only
The lane is ending or you should prepare to exit
options.text_zhHant: 車道即將結束或應準備駛出

questions.explanation_en: Large broken lines mark ending freeway and street lanes. If you are driving in a lane with these markings, be prepared to exit the freeway or for the lane to end.
questions.explanation_zh: 大虚线标记即将结束的高速公路和街道车道。如果你在有这些标记的车道上行驶，请准备驶出高速公路或车道即将结束。
questions.explanation_zhHant: 大虛線標記即將結束的高速公路和街道車道。如果你在有這些標記的車道上行駛，請準備駛出高速公路或車道即將結束。
questions.stem_zhHant: 高速公路車道上的大虛線表示：

questions.stem_en: You may turn right at a red traffic light ONLY after:

options.text_en: Slowing down and checking for traffic
Coming to a complete stop and yielding to pedestrians, unless a No Turn on Red sign is posted
options.text_zhHant: 完全停車並讓行人先行後，除非有禁止紅燈右轉標誌

options.text_en: Honking your horn to warn pedestrians
Waiting for the light to turn yellow

questions.explanation_en: You may turn right at a red light after making a complete stop and yielding to pedestrians and traffic, unless a No Turn on Red sign is posted.
questions.explanation_zh: 你可以在红灯处右转，前提是完全停车并让行行人和交通，除非有禁止红灯右转的标志。
questions.explanation_zhHant: 你可以在紅燈處右轉，前提是完全停車并讓行行人和交通，除非有禁止紅燈右轉的標誌。
questions.stem_zhHant: 紅燈時可以右轉，僅在：

questions.stem_en: You may NOT turn right on a red arrow light. When can you proceed?

options.text_en: After stopping and looking both ways
Only when the light changes to green

questions.explanation_en: You may not turn right at a red arrow. You must wait until the light changes to green before making your turn.
questions.explanation_zh: 在红色箭头灯处不可以右转。你必须等到灯变绿才能转弯。
questions.explanation_zhHant: 在紅色箭頭燈處不可以右轉。你必須等到燈變綠才能轉弯。
questions.stem_zhHant: 紅色箭頭燈時不可右轉。你什麼時候可以通行？

questions.stem_en: At a T-intersection without signs or signals, who has the right-of-way?

options.text_en: The vehicle on the terminating road

options.text_zhHant: 貫通（繼續）道路上的車輛

questions.explanation_en: At a T-intersection without stop or yield signs, vehicles, bicyclists, and pedestrians on the through road (going straight) have the right-of-way.
questions.explanation_zh: 在没有停车或让行标志的T字路口，直行道路上的车辆、骑自行车者和行人拥有先行权。
questions.explanation_zhHant: 在沒有停車或讓行標誌的T字路口，直行道路上的車輛、騎自行車者和行人擁有先行權。
questions.stem_zhHant: 在沒有標誌或信號燈的丁字路口，誰有優先通行權？

questions.stem_en: When entering a roundabout, you must:

options.text_en: Yield to all traffic already in the roundabout
options.text_zhHant: 讓已在環內的所有車輛先行
總是完全停車後再進入

questions.explanation_en: When entering a roundabout, slow down, yield to all traffic already in the roundabout, and enter heading to the right when there is a safe gap.
questions.explanation_zh: 进入环形交叉路口时，减速，让已经在环形交叉路口内的所有交通先行，并在有安全间隙时向右驶入。
questions.explanation_zhHant: 進入環形交叉路口時，減速，讓已經在環形交叉路口內的所有交通先行，并在有安全間隙時向右駛入。
questions.stem_zhHant: 進入環形交叉路口時，你必須：

questions.stem_en: If you miss your exit in a roundabout, you should:

options.text_en: Make a U-turn inside the roundabout
Continue around until you reach your exit again
options.text_zhHant: 繼續繞行直到再次到達你的出口

questions.explanation_en: If you miss your exit in a roundabout, continue driving around the roundabout until you reach your exit again. Do not stop, back up, or reverse.
questions.explanation_zh: 如果你在环形交叉路口错过了出口，继续绕行直到再次到达你的出口。不要停车、倒车或逆行。
questions.explanation_zhHant: 如果你在環形交叉路口錯過了出口，繼續繞行直到再次到達你的出口。不要停車、倒車或逆行。
questions.stem_zhHant: 如果你在環形交叉路口錯過了出口，你應該：

questions.stem_en: You may turn left from a one-way street onto another one-way street at a red light:

options.text_en: Only when a sign permits it
After stopping completely, yielding to traffic and pedestrians, unless a sign prohibits it
options.text_zhHant: 完全停車、讓車輛和行人先行後，除非有禁止標誌

questions.explanation_en: You may turn left from a one-way street onto a one-way street at a red light after stopping completely, yielding to other vehicles and pedestrians, and checking that no sign prohibits the turn.
questions.explanation_zh: 你可以在红灯时从单行道左转进入另一条单行道，前提是完全停车、让行其他车辆和行人，并确认没有禁止转弯的标志。
questions.explanation_zhHant: 你可以在紅燈時從單行道左轉進入另一條單行道，前提是完全停車、讓行其他車輛和行人，并確認沒有禁止轉弯的標誌。
questions.stem_zhHant: 你可以在紅燈時從單行道左轉進入另一條單行道：

questions.stem_en: It is against the law to block an intersection when:

options.text_en: You do not have enough space to completely cross before the light turns red
options.text_zhHant: 你沒有足夠空間在燈變紅前完全通過

questions.explanation_en: Do not enter an intersection if you cannot get completely across before the traffic signal turns red. Blocking an intersection is illegal and can result in a citation.
questions.explanation_zh: 如果你不能在交通信号灯变红前完全通过交叉路口，不要进入。阻塞交叉路口是违法的，可能被开罚单。
questions.explanation_zhHant: 如果你不能在交通信號燈變紅前完全通過交叉路口，不要進入。阻塞交叉路口是違法的，可能被開罰單。
questions.stem_zhHant: 在以下情況下阻塞路口是違法的：

questions.stem_en: It is against the law to follow within how many feet of an emergency vehicle that has its siren and lights on?
questions.explanation_en: It is against the law to follow within 300 feet of any fire engine, law enforcement vehicle, ambulance, or other emergency vehicle when their siren or flashing lights are on.
questions.explanation_zh: 当消防车、执法车辆、救护车或其他紧急车辆的警笛或闪烁灯亮起时，跟在它们300英尺以内是违法的。
questions.explanation_zhHant: 當消防車、執法車輛、救護車或其他緊急車輛的警笛或閃烁燈亮起時，跟在它们300英尺以內是違法的。
questions.stem_zhHant: 跟在開啟警笛和燈光的緊急車輛後方多少英尺以內是違法的？

questions.stem_en: On a two-lane undivided highway, the maximum speed limit is:
questions.explanation_en: The maximum speed limit on a two-lane undivided highway is 55 mph, unless otherwise posted.
questions.explanation_zh: 双车道无分隔公路的最高速度限制为55英里/小时，除非另有标示。
questions.explanation_zhHant: 雙車道無分隔公路的最高速度限制為55英裡/小時，除非另有標示。
questions.stem_zhHant: 雙車道無分隔公路的最高速度限制是：

questions.stem_en: The speed limit at a blind intersection is:
questions.explanation_en: A blind intersection has no stop signs at any corner. If your view is blocked, the speed limit is 15 mph. Move slowly forward until you can see cross traffic.
questions.explanation_zh: 盲区交叉路口在任何角落都没有停车标志。如果你的视线被挡住，速度限制为15英里/小时。慢慢向前移动直到你能看到横向交通。
questions.explanation_zhHant: 盲區交叉路口在任何角落都沒有停車標誌。如果你的视線被擋住，速度限制為15英裡/小時。慢慢向前移動直到你能看到橫向交通。
questions.stem_zhHant: 盲區路口的速度限制是：

questions.stem_en: The speed limit within 100 feet of a railroad crossing where you cannot see tracks for 400 feet in both directions is:
questions.explanation_en: The speed limit is 15 mph within 100 feet of a railroad crossing where you cannot see the tracks for 400 feet in both directions, unless controlled by gates or signals.
questions.explanation_zh: 在铁路道口100英尺范围内，如果你看不到两个方向400英尺的轨道，速度限制为15英里/小时，除非由栏杆或信号灯控制。
questions.explanation_zhHant: 在鐵路道口100英尺範圍內，如果你看不到兩個方向400英尺的軌道，速度限制為15英裡/小時，除非由欄杆或信號燈控制。
questions.stem_zhHant: 在看不到兩個方向400英尺軌道的鐵路道口100英尺範圍內，速度限制是：

questions.stem_en: When towing a trailer on a highway, the maximum speed limit is:
questions.explanation_en: When towing a trailer, the maximum speed limit is 55 mph on all California highways, regardless of the posted speed limit for other vehicles.
questions.explanation_zh: 拖挂拖车时，所有加州高速公路的最高速度限制为55英里/小时，无论其他车辆的标示速度限制是多少。
questions.explanation_zhHant: 拖掛拖車時，所有加州高速公路的最高速度限制為55英裡/小時，無論其他車輛的標示速度限制是多少。
questions.stem_zhHant: 在高速公路上拖拽拖車時，最高速度限制是：

questions.stem_en: You may pass on the right side of another vehicle only when:

options.text_en: You are on a highway with only one lane in each direction
options.text_zhHant: 你在每個方向只有一條車道的公路上

options.text_en: The vehicle ahead is turning left and you can safely pass on the right
options.text_zhHant: 前方車輛正在左轉且你可以安全從右側通過

options.text_en: The road has a solid yellow center line
options.text_zhHant: 道路有黃色實線中心線

options.text_en: The vehicle ahead is going the speed limit
options.text_zhHant: 前方車輛正以限速行駛

questions.explanation_en: You may pass on the right when the driver ahead is turning left and you can safely pass, when there are two or more lanes going your direction, or on a one-way street.
questions.explanation_zh: 当前方驾驶员正在左转且你可以安全通过时、当有两条或更多同向车道时、或在单行道上时，你可以从右侧超车。
questions.explanation_zhHant: 當前方駕駛員正在左轉且你可以安全通過時、當有兩條或更多同向車道時、或在單行道上時，你可以從右側超車。
questions.stem_zhHant: 你只能在以下情況下從右側超車：

questions.stem_en: You should not pass another vehicle within how many feet of an intersection or railroad crossing?
questions.explanation_en: Do not pass within 100 feet of an intersection, bridge, tunnel, railroad crossing, or other hazardous area.
questions.explanation_zh: 不要在交叉路口、桥梁、隧道、铁路道口或其他危险区域100英尺范围内超车。
questions.explanation_zhHant: 不要在交叉路口、橋梁、隧道、鐵路道口或其他危險區域100英尺範圍內超車。
questions.stem_zhHant: 在距路口或鐵路道口多少英尺範圍內不應超車？

questions.stem_en: It is illegal to drive in a bicycle lane unless you are:

options.text_en: Trying to pass a slow vehicle
Parking, entering or leaving the road, or turning within 200 feet of an intersection
options.text_zhHant: 停車、進出道路或在距路口200英尺內轉彎

questions.explanation_en: It is illegal to drive in a bicycle lane unless you are parking (where permitted), entering or leaving the road, or turning within 200 feet of an intersection.
questions.explanation_zh: 除非你正在停车（在允许的地方）、进出道路或在交叉路口200英尺范围内转弯，否则在自行车道上行驶是违法的。
questions.explanation_zhHant: 除非你正在停車（在允許的地方）、進出道路或在交叉路口200英尺範圍內轉弯，否則在自行車道上行駛是違法的。
questions.stem_zhHant: 在自行車道上行駛是違法的，除非你正在：

questions.stem_en: You may drive for a maximum of how many feet in a center left-turn lane?
questions.explanation_en: You may only drive for 200 feet in the center left-turn lane. It is not a regular traffic lane or a passing lane; it is used only to prepare for and make left turns or U-turns.
questions.explanation_zh: 你最多只能在中央左转车道上行驶200英尺。它不是普通的行驶车道或超车车道；它仅用于准备和执行左转或掉头。
questions.explanation_zhHant: 你最多隻能在中央左轉車道上行駛200英尺。它不是普通的行駛車道或超車車道；它僅用於準備和執行左轉或掉頭。
questions.stem_zhHant: 你在中央左轉車道上最多可以行駛多少英尺？

questions.stem_en: A red painted curb means:
questions.explanation_en: A red curb means no stopping, standing, or parking at any time. Buses may stop at red zones marked for buses only.
questions.explanation_zh: 红色路缘表示任何时候都不得停车、站立或停放车辆。公交车可以在仅限公交车的红色区域停靠。
questions.explanation_zhHant: 紅色路緣表示任何時候都不得停車、站立或停放車輛。公交車可以在僅限公交車的紅色區域停靠。
questions.stem_zhHant: 紅色路緣表示：

questions.stem_en: A yellow painted curb means:

options.text_en: Loading and unloading passengers or freight only

questions.explanation_en: A yellow curb means you may stop only long enough to load or unload passengers or freight. Do not stop longer than the time posted. Noncommercial drivers usually must stay with the vehicle.
questions.explanation_zh: 黄色路缘表示你只能停留足够装卸乘客或货物的时间。不要停留超过标示的时间。非商业驾驶员通常必须留在车辆旁边。
questions.explanation_zhHant: 黄色路緣表示你隻能停留足夠裝卸乘客或貨物的時間。不要停留超過標示的時間。非商業駕駛員通常必須留在車輛旁邊。
questions.stem_zhHant: 黃色路緣表示：

questions.stem_en: When driving on a steep narrow mountain road and meeting another vehicle, which vehicle must yield?
questions.explanation_en: On a steep narrow road, the vehicle facing downhill must yield by backing up. The vehicle facing downhill has more control when backing uphill.
questions.explanation_zh: 在陡峭狭窄的道路上，下坡方向的车辆必须倒车让行。下坡方向的车辆在倒车上坡时有更好的控制力。
questions.explanation_zhHant: 在陡峭狹窄的道路上，下坡方向的車輛必須倒車讓行。下坡方向的車輛在倒車上坡時有更好的控制力。
questions.stem_zhHant: 在陡峭狹窄的山路上遇到另一輛車，哪輛車必須讓行？

questions.stem_en: Fines for traffic violations in a highway construction zone when workers are present can be:
questions.explanation_en: Fines are doubled in highway construction or maintenance zones when workers are present. Certain roads designated as Safety Enhanced-Double Fine Zones also have doubled fines.
questions.explanation_zh: 在有工人在场的高速公路施工或养护区域，罚款加倍。某些被指定为安全增强-双倍罚款区的道路也有加倍罚款。
questions.explanation_zhHant: 在有工人在場的高速公路施工或養護區域，罰款加倍。某些被指定為安全增强-雙倍罰款區的道路也有加倍罰款。
questions.stem_zhHant: 有工人在場的高速公路施工區域交通違規罰款可以：

questions.stem_en: When a large truck makes a right turn, it often needs to:

options.text_en: Swing wide to the left before turning right
options.text_zhHant: 先向左大幅擺動再右轉

questions.explanation_en: Large trucks often swing wide to the left to complete a right turn because their rear wheels follow a shorter path than their front wheels. Do not try to squeeze between the truck and the curb.
questions.explanation_zh: 大型卡车在右转时经常需要向左大幅摆动，因为后轮走的路径比前轮短。不要试图从卡车和路缘之间挤过去。
questions.explanation_zhHant: 大型卡車在右轉時經常需要向左大幅擺動，因為後輪走的路徑比前輪短。不要試圖從卡車和路緣之間擠過去。
questions.stem_zhHant: 大型卡車右轉時，通常需要：

questions.stem_en: You should NOT drive next to a large truck for longer than necessary because:

options.text_en: You may be in the truck driver's blind spot and it limits their ability to avoid road hazards
options.text_zhHant: 你可能在卡車駕駛員的盲區中，限制了他們避開路面危險的能力
卡車總是有優先通行權

questions.explanation_en: Driving alongside a large truck places you in their blind spot and makes it hard for the truck driver to avoid dangers. Always pass large trucks on the left and move ahead quickly.
questions.explanation_zh: 在大型卡车旁边行驶会让你处于他们的盲区中，使卡车司机难以避开危险。始终从左侧超越大型卡车并快速通过。
questions.explanation_zhHant: 在大型卡車旁邊行駛會讓你處於他们的盲區中，使卡車司機難以避開危險。始終從左側超越大型卡車并快速通過。
questions.stem_zhHant: 你不應在大型卡車旁邊停留太久，因為：

questions.stem_en: When driving near a light rail vehicle, you should NOT:

options.text_en: Turn in front of an approaching light rail vehicle
options.text_zhHant: 在駛來的輕軌車輛前方轉彎

options.text_en: Yield to the light rail vehicle

questions.explanation_en: Never turn in front of an approaching light rail vehicle. Light rail vehicles have the same rights as other vehicles on public roads, and they cannot stop quickly.
questions.explanation_zh: 永远不要在接近的轻轨车辆前方转弯。轻轨车辆在公共道路上拥有与其他车辆相同的权利，而且它们无法快速停车。
questions.explanation_zhHant: 永遠不要在接近的輕軌車輛前方轉弯。輕軌車輛在公共道路上擁有與其他車輛相同的權利，而且它们無法快速停車。
questions.stem_zhHant: 在輕軌車輛附近駕駛時，你不應該：

questions.stem_en: You should dim your high-beam headlights to low beams within how many feet of an oncoming vehicle?
questions.explanation_en: Dim your high-beam headlights to low beams within 500 feet of an oncoming vehicle and within 300 feet of a vehicle you are following.
questions.explanation_zh: 在迎面来车500英尺范围内和跟车300英尺范围内，将远光灯切换到近光灯。
questions.explanation_zhHant: 在迎面來車500英尺範圍內和跟車300英尺範圍內，將遠光燈切換到近光燈。
questions.stem_zhHant: 在距迎面來車多少英尺範圍內應將遠光燈切換為近光燈？

questions.stem_en: When approaching a stationary emergency vehicle with flashing lights on the side of the road, you must:

options.text_en: Continue driving at the same speed
options.text_zhHant: 保持相同速度繼續行駛

options.text_en: Move over to another lane if possible and slow down

questions.explanation_en: When approaching a stationary emergency vehicle or tow truck displaying flashing lights, move over to another lane if possible and slow down to pass safely.
questions.explanation_zh: 当接近显示闪烁灯光的静止紧急车辆或拖车时，如果可能的话移到另一条车道并减速安全通过。
questions.explanation_zhHant: 當接近显示閃烁燈光的静止緊急車輛或拖車時，如果可能的話移到另一條車道并減速安全通過。
questions.stem_zhHant: 駛近路邊閃燈的停靠緊急車輛時，你必須：

questions.stem_en: A collision must be reported to DMV within 10 days if there is:

options.text_en: Injury, death, or property damage over $1,000
options.text_zhHant: 受傷、死亡或財產損失超過$1,000

options.text_en: Only if a police report was filed
options.text_zhHant: 僅在提交了警察報告的情況下

questions.explanation_en: You must report a collision to DMV within 10 days if anyone was injured, killed, or if property damage exceeds $1,000, regardless of who was at fault.
questions.explanation_zh: 如果有人受伤、死亡或财产损失超过1,000美元，你必须在10天内向DMV报告碰撞事故，无论谁有过错。
questions.explanation_zhHant: 如果有人受傷、死亡或財產损失超過1,000美元，你必須在10天內向DMV報告碰撞事故，無論誰有過錯。
questions.stem_zhHant: 如果有以下情況，碰撞事故必須在10天內向DMV報告：

questions.stem_en: If you refuse to take a chemical test (blood or breath) when lawfully arrested for DUI, your driving privilege will be:

options.text_en: Suspended or revoked for one year

questions.explanation_en: If you refuse to take a chemical test when lawfully arrested for DUI, DMV will suspend or revoke your driving privilege for one year under California's implied consent law.
questions.explanation_zh: 如果你在因酒驾被合法逮捕时拒绝接受化学测试，根据加州的默示同意法，DMV将暂停或吊销你的驾驶特权一年。
questions.explanation_zhHant: 如果你在因酒駕被合法逮捕時拒絕接受化學測試，根据加州的默示同意法，DMV將暫停或吊銷你的駕駛特權一年。
questions.stem_zhHant: 因酒駕被合法逮捕時拒絕接受化學檢測（血液或呼氣），你的駕駛資格將被：

questions.stem_en: It is illegal to carry an open container of alcohol in your vehicle. Where must an opened container be kept?

options.text_en: In the trunk or an area where passengers do not sit
options.text_zhHant: 後備箱或乘客不坐的區域

questions.explanation_en: If you have an open container of alcohol, it must be kept in the trunk or an area where passengers do not sit. It is also illegal to keep an open container in the glove box.
questions.explanation_zh: 如果你有已开封的酒精容器，必须将其放在后备箱或乘客不坐的区域。将开封的容器放在手套箱中也是违法的。
questions.explanation_zhHant: 如果你有已開封的酒精容器，必須將其放在後備箱或乘客不坐的區域。將開封的容器放在手套箱中也是違法的。
questions.stem_zhHant: 在車內攜帶開封的酒精容器是違法的。開封的容器必須放在哪裡？

questions.stem_en: It is illegal to wear headsets or earplugs while driving in:

options.text_en: Headsets are always legal while driving
options.text_zhHant: 駕駛時佩戴耳機始終合法

questions.explanation_en: It is illegal to wear a headset or earplugs in both ears while driving. You may wear one in a single ear. You must be able to hear sirens, horns, and other warning sounds.
questions.explanation_zh: 驾驶时在双耳佩戴耳机或耳塞是违法的。你可以在单耳佩戴。你必须能够听到警笛、喇叭和其他警告声音。
questions.explanation_zhHant: 駕駛時在雙耳佩戴耳機或耳塞是違法的。你可以在單耳佩戴。你必須能夠聽到警笛、喇叭和其他警告聲音。
questions.stem_zhHant: 駕駛時在哪裡佩戴耳機或耳塞是違法的？

questions.stem_en: California law requires minimum liability insurance coverage for bodily injury or death of one person of:
questions.explanation_en: California requires minimum liability insurance of $15,000 for injury or death of one person, $30,000 for injury or death of more than one person, and $5,000 for property damage.
questions.explanation_zh: 加州要求最低责任保险为：单人伤亡15,000美元，多人伤亡30,000美元，财产损失5,000美元。
questions.explanation_zhHant: 加州要求最低責任保險為：單人傷亡15,000美元，多人傷亡30,000美元，財產损失5,000美元。
questions.stem_zhHant: 加州法律要求單人人身傷害或死亡的最低責任保險額度為：

questions.stem_en: If your vehicle stalls on railroad tracks and a train is approaching, you should:

options.text_en: Stay in the vehicle and try to restart it
options.text_zhHant: 留在車內嘗試重新啟動

options.text_en: Exit immediately and run diagonally away from the tracks in the direction the train is coming from
options.text_zhHant: 立即下車，沿火車駛來的方向斜向遠離鐵軌跑

options.text_en: Wave at the train to stop
Push the vehicle off the tracks

questions.explanation_en: If your vehicle stalls on railroad tracks and a train is approaching, exit immediately and run diagonally away from the tracks in the direction the train is coming from. Then call 911.
questions.explanation_zh: 如果你的车辆在铁轨上熄火且火车正在驶来，立即下车并沿着火车驶来的方向斜向远离铁轨跑。然后拨打911。
questions.explanation_zhHant: 如果你的車輛在鐵軌上熄火且火車正在駛來，立即下車并沿着火車駛來的方向斜向遠離鐵軌跑。然後撥打911。
questions.stem_zhHant: 如果你的車輛在鐵軌上熄火且火車正在駛來，你應該：

questions.stem_en: A minor moving to California from another state must:
questions.explanation_en: When you move to California, you must get a California driver license.
questions.explanation_zh: 搬到加州后，必须获得加州驾照。

questions.stem_en: For the drive test, the vehicle must have tires with at least:
questions.explanation_en: Tires must have at least 1/32 inch of uniform tread depth. Donut tires are not allowed.
questions.explanation_zh: 轮胎必须有至少1/32英寸的均匀胎面深度。备用小轮胎不允许使用。

questions.stem_en: The horn on a vehicle used for the drive test must be audible from at least:
questions.explanation_en: The horn must be loud enough to be heard from at least 200 feet.
questions.explanation_zh: 喇叭声音必须能在至少200英尺外听到。

questions.stem_en: Before the drive test begins, the examiner will check:

options.text_en: Vehicle safety features like mirrors, lights, and brakes

questions.explanation_en: The examiner will ask you to demonstrate vehicle controls including mirrors, brake lights, horn, and turn signals.
questions.explanation_zh: 考官会要求你演示车辆控制装置，包括后视镜、刹车灯、喇叭和转向灯。

questions.stem_en: A rental vehicle can be used for the drive test if:

options.text_en: Your name is on the rental contract and it allows drive tests
The rental company approves by phone

questions.explanation_en: Your name must be on the rental contract, and the contract must not exclude behind-the-wheel tests.
questions.explanation_zh: 你的名字必须在租车合同上，且合同不得排除路考使用。

questions.stem_en: Interpreters during the drive test are:

options.text_en: Allowed only during the pre-drive inspection

questions.explanation_en: Interpreters may only be used during the pre-drive inspection, not during the actual driving test.
questions.explanation_zh: 口译员只能在路考前检查时使用，不能在实际驾驶考试期间使用。

questions.stem_en: The foot brake pedal must have at least how much clearance from the floorboard?
questions.explanation_en: There must be at least 1 inch of clearance between the brake pedal and the floorboard when depressed.
questions.explanation_zh: 刹车踏板踩下时与地板之间至少要有1英寸的间隙。

questions.stem_en: During the pre-drive inspection, you must show at least how many rearview mirrors?
questions.explanation_en: You need at least two rearview mirrors, one of which must be on the left side.
questions.explanation_zh: 至少需要两面后视镜，其中一面必须在车辆左侧。

questions.stem_en: The driver window on the test vehicle must:
questions.explanation_en: The window on the driver side must be able to open.
questions.explanation_zh: 驾驶员侧的车窗必须能够打开。

questions.stem_en: If your test vehicle doesn't meet requirements, your drive test will be:
questions.explanation_en: If your vehicle does not meet the requirements, your drive test will be rescheduled.
questions.explanation_zh: 如果车辆不符合要求，路考将被重新安排。
